key_insight: Increasing barrel filling ratio from 43% to 53% made detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coatings denser and more wear-resistant, despite a slight rise in friction.
engineering_relevance: Optimizing spraying fill ratio is a practical way to improve the durability of protective carbide coatings for high-wear, high-temperature machine components.
method: Detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coatings were deposited at barrel filling ratios of 43% and 53% under identical conditions.
Microstructure and phases were examined using microscopy and X-ray diffraction.
Tribological behavior was measured in dry ball-on-disc tests at 10 and 15 N and 5 and 10 cm/s.
evidence_signal: The 53% fill ratio coating showed a denser structure and achieved a minimum wear rate of 1.23 × 10^-4 mm3/(m·N), indicating improved wear resistance.
watch_item: The higher-fill coating improved wear resistance but also showed a higher coefficient of friction, so tribological trade-offs should be considered for real applications.
paper_title: Effect of Barrel Filling Ratio on the Microstructure, Phase Composition and Tribological Performance of Detonation-Sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr Coatings
